text:
RECITALS

An issue around parentage has been raised in connection to a Child Arrangements order which the court needs a report to ascertain whether [putative father’s name] is the father of [name of child(ren)].

A tester needs samples from [putative father’s name] and [name of child(ren)] so that they can carry out their tests and prepare a report.

IT IS ORDERED THAT:

The court officer must complete Parts I and II of the standard court form BD1 and send this to [(cafcass) DNArequests@cafcass.gov.uk and inboundpost@cafcass.gov.uk] / [cafcass cymru) CafcasscymruCAT@gov.wales]

A [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru] contracted officer must then make arrangements with [putative father’s name] and [name of person with care and control of child(ren)] for mouth samples to be given by [name of child(ren)] and [putative father’s name] supervised by an officer commissioned by [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru].

[Putative father’s name] and [name of person with care and control of child(ren)] must allow the officer of [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru] to take photographic identity of them at the time the sample is taken.

The [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru] contracted officer must send the samples to be tested to arrive by [date [(no later than roughly 6 weeks from the date a valid dna order & bd1 are sent to cafcass/cafcass cymru)].

The tester must send the written test report to the courts and the courts only by [date [(roughly 6 weeks from the date a valid dna order & bd1 are sent to cafcass/cafcass cymru)].

The costs of the sampling, testing and report with be paid for by Cafcass in respect of proceedings in England and by the Ministry of Justice in respect of proceedings in Wales.

Dated [date]

Order for DNA testing by Cafcass
Children Act 1989 | Order for DNA testing by Cafcass
Children Act 1989 | Order for DNA testing by Cafcass
Children Act 1989
